body.title: Nanny Ogg: wire OS cron backend + fascia slash commands
body.result: cron_backend.py (abstract+Linux+Windows stub), /api/nanny/cron REST API, fascia cron panel with add/disable/enable/run — 16 tests
body.intention: I intend that Nanny Ogg manages OS-level cron jobs, exposing them in her fascia with slash commands for list/add/disable/enable/run-now — no inference involved.
body.description:
Wire Nanny Ogg to the OS cron backend (crontab on Linux; Task Scheduler abstraction on Windows). Expose job management via fascia Settings box slash commands. Use an abstraction layer so the backend is swappable.

**Affected files:** devices/nanny_ogg/cron_backend.py (new), devices/nanny_ogg/device.py (update health/status), devices/web_server/server.py (Nanny Ogg fascia Settings box)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; no inference in scheduling path; Windows compatibility via abstraction layer
**Scope boundary:** OS cron read/write only. No rules engine (separate ticket if needed). Slash commands: /cron list, /cron add <expr> <cmd>, /cron disable <id>, /cron enable <id>, /cron run-now <id>. Out of scope: complex graph-tree rules engine.
**Completion criteria:** /cron list in the fascia returns current crontab entries; /cron add '* * * * * echo test' writes a crontab entry; /cron disable <id> comments it out; /cron enable <id> restores it; cron_backend.py has an abstract base class with Linux + stub-Windows implementations.
